strategies.name: QQQ Daily Put Credit Spread (80%+ Probability of Profit)
strategies.description: A simple daily options income strategy selling $1-wide put credit spreads on QQQ at strikes with 80%+ probability of profit. Select a short-dated expiration, sell a put below current price at the 80%+ probability level, buy protective put $1 below. Scale contracts to reach daily income target. No technical analysis or directional bias used — purely probability-based strike selection. The strategy is extremely simplified and missing critical risk management details.

entry_signals.condition: Select a put strike with 80%+ probability of profit as displayed on the brokerage options chain. This corresponds to approximately 15-20 delta.
Sell the selected put strike, buy a put $1 below for protection. This creates a 1-point wide bull put spread.

parameters.example: sell 481 put / buy 480 put when QQQ above 481

entry_signals.condition: Scale the number of contracts to hit your daily income target.

parameters.scaling_method: target income / credit per contract = number of contracts

exit_rules.condition: Let both puts expire worthless if QQQ stays above the sold strike at expiration. Max profit is the total credit received.

parameters.condition: QQQ closes above short put strike

exit_rules.condition: NOT SPECIFIED — this is a critical gap in the strategy as presented

parameters.warning: no stop-loss or management rules provided for losing trades
parameters.max_possible_loss: $1 spread width - credit received, per contract

risk_management.position_size: scale contracts to daily income target (example: 6 contracts for ~$100)
risk_management.risk_reward_ratio: approximately 1:5 unfavorable (risk $83 to make $17 per contract) — mitigated by 80%+ win rate
additional_rules: CRITICAL: No stop-loss rules provided — must be added before live trading

filters.probability_minimum: 80%+ probability of profit on options chain
filters.no_technical_filters: purely probability-based — no chart analysis, VIX filter, or trend filter

missing_elements: Stop-loss rules (CRITICAL — no guidance on managing losing trades)
DTE selection criteria (same-day vs next-day vs longer)
Position sizing relative to account size
What to do when QQQ gaps through the strike
Any technical analysis or directional bias
Management of trades that are ITM before expiration
assumptions: 80% probability of profit is a reasonable minimum
Brokerage displays probability of profit on options chain
Trader monitors and can exit if needed
Strategy repeated daily for income generation
source_quotes: Go on QQQ, pick whatever strike date you want
Find a put with over 80% probability of profit
Sell that put, and then buy one $1 below
Scale it to 6 contracts and you've got $101

strategy_names: QQQ Daily Put Credit Spread (80%+ Probability of Profit)
summary.extraction_notes: Extremely simplified 55-second YouTube Short. Only covers basic spread construction. Missing ALL risk management (no stop-loss, no daily max loss, no market filters). The strategy has an unfavorable risk/reward per trade (~1:5) that relies entirely on high win rate. Must add significant risk management before any implementation. Low confidence due to missing critical details.
summary.video_title: How To Grow $100 Daily Trading QQQ
summary.primary_trading_style: Daily options income via put credit spread selling
summary.automation_feasibility: HIGH — Strike selection by probability/delta is a standard options chain query. Spread construction ($1 wide) is trivial. Contract scaling is arithmetic. The main implementation challenge is that the strategy lacks stop-loss rules — must be designed before automation. Consider adding: VIX filter, time-of-day entry window, and max loss exit at 2x credit received.
summary.key_insight: Put credit spreads on QQQ can generate consistent small daily income if the 80% probability threshold is maintained. However, the 1:5 risk/reward means a single losing trade wipes out approximately 5 winning trades — making risk management (which is entirely absent from this video) the most critical factor for long-term profitability.
